fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Washington Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity washington, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to washington, speech-language pathologist compact washington, washington speech-language pathologist endorsement application

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in Washington.

license_types.title: Interim SLP Permit
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship in Washington.

steps.title: Complete Education and Clinical Practicum
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or doctorate in speech-language pathology from an accredited educational institution and complete the required clinical practicum.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology administered by the Educational Testing Service (ETS).

steps.title: Apply for Licensure
steps.description: Submit your application, official transcripts, verification of supervised professional experience, and examination scores to the Washington State Department of Health.

documents.detail: Washington State Patrol and FBI background check.

telepractice_text: Washington permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Washington issues an Interim SLP Permit for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Washington is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Washington SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: $175 initial. $100 annual renewal (Washington uses annual renewal on your birthday).

faqs.question: Is Washington part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Washington allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Washington have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Washington participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Washington without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Washington?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Washington is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Washington
hero.dek: Washington Speech-Language Pathologist license requires a master's degree, passing the national exam, and completion of a supervised professional experience.
